channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FWO restarts stalled Karachi Red Line BRT work as Sindh government sets 90-day deadline
item.description: Work on Karachi’s long-delayed Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) Red Line project has been restarted, with the Frontier Works Organisation (FWO) taking charge of construction activities on University Road. Kara...

item.title: President Ruto Jokes About Nigeria's English, Responding to Tinubu's Remarks
item.description: Kenya's President William Ruto took a lighthearted jab at Nigeria’s spoken English during a recent event, claiming that it often requires a translator. His playful comment came as a response to Nigeri...

item.title: LTFRB distributes over P27 million in fuel aid to taxi drivers in final payout
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) announced that over P27 million in fuel subsidies was distributed to taxi drivers on the second and final day of payouts under the gove...

item.title: Starmer's Future as Prime Minister Under Threat Amid Labour Party Struggles and Upcoming Elections
item.description: Keir Starmer, the Prime Minister of the UK, has claimed he intends to remain in power into the 2030s, despite growing unrest within the Labour Party. Starmer’s leadership faces serious scrutiny, parti...

item.title: Dollar Discusses Why Njugush and Wakavinye’s Marriage Ended and Advice for Couples
item.description: Businessman Dollar Furniture has shared his perspective on the breakup of comedian Timothy Kimani, popularly known as Njugush, and his long-term partner Celestine Ndinda. The couple, together for 14 y...

item.title: US-Iran talks fail as diplomatic efforts collapse
item.description: Diplomatic efforts to end the ongoing conflict between the US-Israeli alliance and Iran have hit a roadblock as peace talks have collapsed. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi returned from Pakista...

item.title: Nigeria spends $400 million on tomato paste imports due to weak processing and post-harvest losses
item.description: Even though Nigeria dey produce millions of tonnes of fresh tomatoes every year, the country still dey spend over $400 million annually dey import tomato paste. The wahala no be production; na because...

item.title: Fire at RAF Fairford Base Raises Concerns Amidst US Operations
item.description: A fire has erupted at a commercial building within RAF Fairford, a Royal Air Force base located near the Wiltshire border in Gloucestershire, England. Gloucestershire Fire and Rescue Service (GFRS) is...

item.title: Erin O’Toole urges cautious and strategic approach to Canada-China relations under Carney
item.description: Former Conservative leader Erin O’Toole is urging Prime Minister Mark Carney to take a measured and cautious approach in managing Canada’s relationship with China, reflecting ongoing concerns about na...

item.title: Brush Fire in Carbon Canyon, Orange County Prompts Evacuations
item.description: On Saturday, April 25, 2026, a brush fire erupted in the Carbon Canyon area of unincorporated Brea, Orange County, prompting evacuation warnings for several nearby communities. Reported shortly after ...

item.title: Ombudsman to pursue charges over leaked identities of investigators and warns of legal consequences
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman strongly denounced the unauthorized online release of the identities of its investigators, describing the act as malicious and dangerous. According to the agency, the leak ...

item.title: MSF warns malaria worsens malnutrition among children in Nigeria
item.description: According to Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), malaria dey one of di top illnesses wey dey affect malnourished children for Nigeria. Alibaba Nuraddeen, wey be acting Medical Team Leader for MSF Katsina,...

item.title: Gaza Holds Historic Local Elections Amid Ongoing Crisis
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza and the West Bank have cast their votes in local elections, the first in two decades. Despite the ongoing humanitarian crisis in Gaza, where the city of Deir al-Balah saw a low vo...

item.title: Parliamentary Chair Calls for Review of South Africa's Draft AI Policy Following Integrity Concerns
item.description: Parliamentary portfolio committee chairperson Khusela Diko has urged Communications and Digital Technologies Minister Solly Malatsi to withdraw the draft national AI policy after its credibility was q...

item.title: Nasir Ja’oji Declares Bid for Tarauni Federal Constituency Seat in 2027 Elections
item.description: Former Senior Special Assistant to President Bola Tinubu on Citizenship and Leadership, Nasir Ja’oji, don officially declare say he wan contest for House of Representatives seat to represent Tarauni F...

item.title: Study suggests EPA in fish oil may impair brain repair after repeated head injuries
item.description: A new study from the Medical University of South Carolina challenges the widely held belief that fish oil supplements are universally beneficial for brain health. Researchers found that eicosapentaeno...

item.title: Annavaasal Dusk Meal Mission Reaches 25 Lakh People After 1000 Days
item.description: The Annavaasal Dusk Meal Mission, a community-driven initiative in Tamil Nadu, has completed 1000 days of uninterrupted service, feeding over 25 lakh people in total. Launched to ensure no one sleeps ...

item.title: Factors Affecting Car Insurance Premiums in South Africa
item.description: Two motorists can own the same make and model of car and still end up paying very different insurance premiums. This is because insurers consider more than just the vehicle; they also assess the indiv...

item.title: Security concerns raised after attempted shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner ahead of King Charles' US visit
item.description: The attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner has triggered serious concerns over security, particularly in relation to King Charles and Queen Camilla's upcoming state visit to the ...

item.title: Veterans of Afghanistan and Gulf War Seek Recognition as Official War Veterans by Parliament
item.description: Veterans of the Afghanistan and Gulf Wars are expressing hope that the Canadian Parliament will finally recognize them as official ‘war veterans’. After decades of advocacy, these veterans believe tha...
